title: Prohibition on Media Interactions for Officials
note: On May 21, 2026, the West Bengal government altered the controversial media gag order following public backlash and opposition protests. A revised memorandum, signed by Additional Chief Secretary Rajesh Pandey, narrowed the scope of the restriction to apply only to regular establishments and parastatals under direct state administrative control.

additionalNotes.label: Deep Dive
additionalNotes.text: For a factual, source-based deep-dive into Circular No. 139-CS/2026—including its complete legal background, political reactions, and comparisons with similar rules in other states—read our complete Factual Deep-Dive Article.

title: Mandatory Vande Mataram in Madrasas
note: The West Bengal Directorate of Madrasa Education issued a formal administrative order making the singing of 'Vande Mataram' compulsory during assembly prayers prior to the commencement of classes in all government-aided, recognized, and model madrasas across the state with immediate effect.

title: Prohibition on Media Interactions for Officials
note: On May 21, 2026, West Bengal Chief Secretary Manoj Agarwal issued a formal administrative circular completely prohibiting government officials, civil servants, and police officers from communicating any document or information to the media without prior official sanction. Invoking multiple service rules, including the All India Services (AIS) Conduct Rules, 1968, the circular bans employees from participating in radio or media programs, writing articles/letters for publications, or making adverse criticisms of state or central government policies.

additionalNotes.label: Deep Dive
additionalNotes.text: For a factual, source-based deep-dive into Circular No. 139-CS/2026—including its complete legal background, political reactions, and comparisons with similar rules in other states—read our complete Factual Deep-Dive Article.

additionalNotes.label: Criticism
additionalNotes.text: The circular drew strong opposition from political and civil society circles. TMC General Secretary Abhishek Banerjee termed it a "gag order" designed to enforce silence. Additionally, associations of school and university teachers strongly condemned the directive as "undemocratic" and an unprecedented "fatwa" aimed at stifling their voices, arguing that it violates the constitutional right to freedom of expression.

title: Assembly Live Streaming Proposal
note: Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari proposed live streaming of Question Hour, debates, bills, and budget sessions to ensure transparency. He urged all parties to maintain decorum and stated that opposition members will be given adequate opportunity to participate.

title: Handover of NH Stretches to NHAI
note: West Bengal government approved the handover of seven national highway stretches (totaling ~196 km) to NHAI and NHIDCL. The stretches include NH-116 and NH-131A for NHAI, and segments in North Bengal for NHIDCL, aimed at facilitating modernization and widening projects.

title: Caste Certificate Re-verification
note: West Bengal government ordered the re-verification of all caste certificates (SC/ST/OBC) issued since 2011 to identify and cancel fraudulent ones issued under the previous administration.

title: Mandatory Vande Mataram in Schools
note: The School Education Department issued an order making the singing of 'Vande Mataram' mandatory during assembly prayers in all schools across West Bengal with immediate effect.

title: Geo-tagged Garbage Complaint App
note: A new technology-driven mobile app was announced to allow citizens to report garbage accumulation in real-time. Geo-tagging will alert civic authorities, with a target cleanup time of 1-2 hours.
